fixture.description: A handler joins a user-controlled file name onto a root directory and reads it with no validation, so input like ../../etc/passwd escapes the root (path traversal).

grader.rubric: PASS if the report flags that readUserFile joins the user-controlled `name` onto ROOT and reads it without any path-traversal validation, so input such as `../../etc/passwd` escapes ROOT and reads arbitrary files (a path-traversal / directory-traversal vulnerability). It should recommend normalizing and verifying the resolved path stays under ROOT (or rejecting `..`). Severity IMPORTANT or CRITICAL. FAIL if the path-traversal risk is not surfaced.

expected_behavior: Security agent flags unvalidated user-controlled path joined to ROOT
expected_not_behavior: Does NOT require a proof-of-concept exploit to flag it
Does NOT dismiss it because ROOT is a constant
